content.text: Blueprint Assignments

DEPRECATED: Azure Blueprints retired July 11, 2026. After retirement, existing assignments retain deployed resources but lose lock enforcement and DINE policy effect. No new assignments can be created after the retirement date.

A blueprint assignment links a published blueprint version to a specific target subscription. The assignment object stores the version ID, parameter values, resource group name mappings, lock mode, and managed identity used to deploy artifacts. Assignments go through a provisioning state machine: Waiting → Creating → Succeeded/Failed → Locking → Locked. The lock mode (None, AllResourcesDoNotDelete, AllResourcesReadOnly) applies to all blueprint-managed resources after the Succeeded state completes. Deleting an assignment removes the locks and the assignment object but does not delete the deployed resources.

attrs.alt: Blueprint assignment states, three lock modes, and system-assigned vs user-assigned identity options

content.text: Creating an Assignment

Creating an assignment requires the published blueprint version ID (the full resource path ending in /versions/<version>), the target subscription, a location for the assignment metadata resource, lock mode, identity type, resource group name mappings (placeholder name → actual RG name/location), and parameter values for each blueprint parameter. The assignment location does not affect where resources are deployed — it controls where the assignment management resource is stored. Use a region close to the target subscription for lower latency.

# Create and manage blueprint assignments
# NOTE: Azure Blueprints retired July 11, 2026. No new assignments after retirement.

MG_ID='alz-landing-zones'
BP_NAME='landing-zone-baseline'
TARGET_SUB='<landing-zone-sub-id>'
BP_VERSION='1.0'
BLUEPRINT_ID="/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/$MG_ID/providers/Microsoft.Blueprint/blueprints/$BP_NAME/versions/$BP_VERSION"

# Create assignment with system-assigned identity and DoNotDelete lock
az blueprint assignment create \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --blueprint-version "$BLUEPRINT_ID" \
  --location eastus \
  --lock-mode AllResourcesDoNotDelete \
  --identity-type SystemAssigned \
  --resource-groups '{
    "rg-workloads": {"name": "rg-payments-prod-eastus", "location": "eastus"},
    "rg-monitoring": {"name": "rg-payments-monitoring-eastus", "location": "eastus"}
  }' \
  --parameters '{
    "subscriptionLocation": {"value": "eastus"},
    "logAnalyticsWorkspaceId": {"value": "/subscriptions/<mgmt-sub>/resourceGroups/rg-management-prod-eastus/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/log-management-prod-eastus"},
    "appTeamGroupObjectId": {"value": "<app-team-group-oid>"}
  }'

# Monitor the assignment until succeeded
while true; do
  STATUS=$(az blueprint assignment show \
    --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
    --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
    --query 'provisioningState' -o tsv)
  echo "$(date -u +%H:%M:%S) Assignment state: $STATUS"
  [[ "$STATUS" == 'succeeded' || "$STATUS" == 'failed' ]] && break
  sleep 30
done

# If failed, check the assignment details for error messages
az blueprint assignment show \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --query 'status' -o json
Updating an Assignment to a New Version

To apply a new blueprint version to an existing assignment, run az blueprint assignment update with the new version ID. The update re-runs all artifact deployments against the target subscription — ARM template artifacts are idempotent and the update only changes resources that differ from the new template. Policy assignment artifacts may add or change policy assignments. Role assignment artifacts may add new role grants. The lock mode persists from the existing assignment unless explicitly changed in the update command.

# Update a blueprint assignment to a new version
# Use when a new blueprint version has been published with updated artifacts.

TARGET_SUB='<landing-zone-sub-id>'
MG_ID='alz-landing-zones'
BP_NAME='landing-zone-baseline'

# Check current assigned version
az blueprint assignment show \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --query 'blueprintId' -o tsv

# Update to version 1.1 (must be published first)
NEW_BP_ID="/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/$MG_ID/providers/Microsoft.Blueprint/blueprints/$BP_NAME/versions/1.1"

az blueprint assignment update \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --blueprint-version "$NEW_BP_ID" \
  --lock-mode AllResourcesDoNotDelete
  # Parameters not specified here inherit from the existing assignment

# Monitor update
az blueprint assignment show \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --query '{Version:blueprintId,Status:provisioningState}' -o json

# List all assignments across subscriptions you have access to
# (Uses Azure Resource Graph — covers all reachable subscriptions)
az graph query -q "
  BlueprintResources
  | where type =~ 'microsoft.blueprint/blueprintassignments'
  | extend
      assignedVersion = tostring(properties.blueprintId),
      lockMode = tostring(properties.locks.mode),
      state = tostring(properties.provisioningState)
  | project subscriptionId, name, assignedVersion, lockMode, state
  | order by subscriptionId asc
" --query 'data[]' -o table
User-Assigned Managed Identity

By default, blueprint assignments use a system-assigned managed identity that Blueprint service automatically grants Owner on the target subscription. Use a user-assigned managed identity (UAMI) when you need the deployment identity to have permissions in multiple subscriptions (for cross-subscription DINE policy remediation), when you want the identity to persist independently of the assignment lifecycle, or when your organization policy prevents automatic Owner grants. Create the UAMI in the management subscription, grant it Owner on the target subscription, then reference it in the assignment via --identity-type UserAssigned.

# Blueprint assignment with User-Assigned Managed Identity (UAMI)
# Use UAMI when DINE-effect policy artifacts need cross-subscription deployment,
# or when you want the identity to persist independently of the assignment.

TARGET_SUB='<landing-zone-sub-id>'
MGMT_SUB='<management-sub-id>'
MG_ID='alz-landing-zones'
BP_NAME='landing-zone-baseline'
RG_UAMI='rg-blueprint-identities'
UAMI_NAME='uami-blueprint-deployment'

# 1. Create the User-Assigned Managed Identity in the management subscription
UAMI_ID=$(az identity create \
  --name $UAMI_NAME \
  --resource-group $RG_UAMI \
  --subscription $MGMT_SUB \
  --query id -o tsv)

UAMI_PRINCIPAL_ID=$(az identity show \
  --ids $UAMI_ID \
  --query principalId -o tsv)

# 2. Grant the UAMI Owner on the target landing zone subscription
#    (Blueprint service grants Owner for system-assigned; do this manually for UAMI)
az role assignment create \
  --role Owner \
  --assignee-object-id $UAMI_PRINCIPAL_ID \
  --assignee-principal-type ServicePrincipal \
  --scope "/subscriptions/$TARGET_SUB"

# 3. Assign the blueprint using the UAMI
BP_VERSION_ID="/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/$MG_ID/providers/Microsoft.Blueprint/blueprints/$BP_NAME/versions/1.0"

az blueprint assignment create \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --blueprint-version "$BP_VERSION_ID" \
  --location eastus \
  --lock-mode AllResourcesDoNotDelete \
  --identity-type UserAssigned \
  --user-assigned-identity "$UAMI_ID" \
  --resource-groups '{
    "rg-workloads": {"name": "rg-payments-prod-eastus", "location": "eastus"}
  }' \
  --parameters '{
    "subscriptionLocation": {"value": "eastus"},
    "appTeamGroupObjectId": {"value": "<app-team-oid>"}
  }'
Deleting an Assignment

Deleting a blueprint assignment removes the assignment object and all resource locks applied by the lock mode. It does NOT delete the resources deployed by the blueprint — virtual machines, storage accounts, resource groups, policy assignments, and role assignments all remain. After deletion, the resources become unmanaged: they can be modified or deleted by anyone with sufficient RBAC access. This is the correct behavior for decommissioning a landing zone template — resources are kept alive while governance guardrails are removed.

# Remove a blueprint assignment (does NOT delete the deployed resources)
# Blueprint assignment removal only removes the assignment object and resource locks.
# Resources deployed by the blueprint remain in the subscription.

TARGET_SUB='<landing-zone-sub-id>'

# Verify what will be un-locked (resources protected by DoNotDelete lose the lock)
az blueprint assignment show \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --query '{LockMode:locks.mode, Resources:resourceGroups}' -o json

# Delete the assignment — removes the assignment object and all blueprint locks
# Resources (VMs, storage, policy assignments, role assignments) are NOT deleted
az blueprint assignment delete \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --yes

# Confirm deletion — command should return an error (assignment not found)
az blueprint assignment show \
  --name 'assign-landing-zone-baseline' \
  --subscription $TARGET_SUB 2>&1 || echo 'Assignment deleted successfully'

# After deletion, clean up resources manually if needed
# Policy assignments deployed by the blueprint remain and must be removed separately:
az policy assignment list --subscription $TARGET_SUB \
  --query '[?displayName!=null].{Name:name, DisplayName:displayName}' -o table

# Remove specific policy assignment from blueprint artifact (no longer blueprint-managed)
az policy assignment delete \
  --name 'require-https-storage' \
  --scope "/subscriptions/$TARGET_SUB"
Assignment Behavior Reference
Scenario
Effect on Resources
Action Required
Assignment deleted (pre-retirement)
Resources persist; locks removed; DINE policy effects continue via existing policy assignments
Remove policy assignments manually if governance should stop
Assignment update to new version
ARM templates re-apply (idempotent); new policy assignments added; existing role assignments unchanged unless removed from artifact
Run update; monitor state; verify new artifacts deployed
Lock mode: AllResourcesDoNotDelete
Prevents delete operations on all blueprint-managed resources (including by Owner)
Use for prod; allows config changes; blocks decommission until assignment is deleted
Lock mode: AllResourcesReadOnly
Prevents all write operations (modify + delete) on blueprint-managed resources
Use for compliance-frozen environments; blocks normal operations — only Blueprint Operator role can update
Blueprint retirement (July 11, 2026)
Locks removed; DINE effects stop; assignment visible but cannot be updated; no new assignments possible
Migrate to Deployment Stacks with denySettings before retirement date
Failed assignment
Partially deployed artifacts remain; locks not applied; re-run or delete and recreate
Check status.message in assignment output for artifact-specific errors
Assigning at Scale

When onboarding many landing zone subscriptions, use a CSV-driven loop to create assignments programmatically. Each subscription gets its own assignment object with subscription-specific parameter values (location, resource group names, app team group OID). The assignment name incorporates the subscription ID to make it unique and traceable. Poll all assignments in a second pass to confirm all reached succeeded before proceeding with workload onboarding.

# Automate blueprint assignments at scale — assign to many subscriptions
# Use this pattern when onboarding new landing zone subscriptions from a pipeline.

MG_ID='alz-landing-zones'
BP_NAME='landing-zone-baseline'
BP_VERSION='1.0'
BLUEPRINT_ID="/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/$MG_ID/providers/Microsoft.Blueprint/blueprints/$BP_NAME/versions/$BP_VERSION"

# Read target subscriptions from a CSV (sub_id,location,app_team_oid,rg_prefix)
# sub-001,eastus,<oid-a>,rg-payments
# sub-002,westus2,<oid-b>,rg-hr

while IFS=',' read -r SUB_ID LOCATION APP_OID RG_PREFIX; do
  echo "Assigning blueprint to $SUB_ID in $LOCATION"

  az blueprint assignment create \
    --name "assign-baseline-$SUB_ID" \
    --subscription "$SUB_ID" \
    --blueprint-version "$BLUEPRINT_ID" \
    --location "$LOCATION" \
    --lock-mode AllResourcesDoNotDelete \
    --identity-type SystemAssigned \
    --resource-groups "{
      \"rg-workloads\": {\"name\": \"${RG_PREFIX}-prod-${LOCATION}\", \"location\": \"${LOCATION}\"},
      \"rg-monitoring\": {\"name\": \"${RG_PREFIX}-monitoring-${LOCATION}\", \"location\": \"${LOCATION}\"}
    }" \
    --parameters "{
      \"subscriptionLocation\": {\"value\": \"${LOCATION}\"},
      \"logAnalyticsWorkspaceId\": {\"value\": \"/subscriptions/<mgmt-sub>/resourceGroups/rg-management-prod-eastus/providers/Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces/log-management-prod-eastus\"},
      \"appTeamGroupObjectId\": {\"value\": \"${APP_OID}\"}
    }"

done < subscriptions.csv

# Poll all assignments until all reach succeeded or failed
while IFS=',' read -r SUB_ID LOCATION APP_OID RG_PREFIX; do
  STATUS=$(az blueprint assignment show \
    --name "assign-baseline-$SUB_ID" \
    --subscription "$SUB_ID" \
    --query 'provisioningState' -o tsv 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown')
  echo "$SUB_ID: $STATUS"
done < subscriptions.csv
Auditing Assignments with Resource Graph

Four Resource Graph queries cover the critical assignment audit scenarios: all assignments with version and lock mode (compliance baseline), stale assignments not on the latest blueprint version (detect subscriptions that missed an update), subscriptions in the landing-zones MG without any assignment (missed onboarding), and assignments in failed state (need remediation). Run these weekly as part of the landing zone governance report and file incidents for subscriptions in the third and fourth categories.

# Audit blueprint assignments across your tenant using Resource Graph

# 1. All assignments with version and lock mode
az graph query -q "
  BlueprintResources
  | where type =~ 'microsoft.blueprint/blueprintassignments'
  | extend
    bpId = tostring(properties.blueprintId),
    lockMode = tostring(properties.locks.mode),
    state = tostring(properties.provisioningState)
  | parse bpId with * '/blueprints/' bpName '/versions/' bpVersion
  | project subscriptionId, name, bpName, bpVersion, lockMode, state
  | order by subscriptionId, name asc
" --query 'data[]' -o table

# 2. Assignments not on the latest version (detect stale assignments)
# First, find the latest version of each blueprint definition:
az blueprint version list \
  --blueprint-name landing-zone-baseline \
  --management-group alz-landing-zones \
  --query '[].{Version:name, Published:status}' -o table

# Then compare each assignment's blueprintId against latest version.
# Assignments on older versions should be updated.

# 3. Subscriptions in the alz-landing-zones MG without a blueprint assignment
# (identifies onboarded subscriptions that missed the automation pipeline)
az graph query -q "
  ResourceContainers
  | where type =~ 'microsoft.resources/subscriptions'
  | where managementGroupAncestorsChain has 'alz-landing-zones'
  | project subId = subscriptionId, subName = name
  | join kind=leftouter (
    BlueprintResources
    | where type =~ 'microsoft.blueprint/blueprintassignments'
    | project subId = subscriptionId, assignmentName = name
  ) on subId
  | where isnull(assignmentName)
  | project subId, subName, Issue='No blueprint assignment found'
" --query 'data[]' -o table

# 4. Assignments that are currently in 'failed' state
az graph query -q "
  BlueprintResources
  | where type =~ 'microsoft.blueprint/blueprintassignments'
  | where tostring(properties.provisioningState) =~ 'failed'
  | project subscriptionId, name, error=tostring(properties.status.message)
" --query 'data[]' -o json
Blueprint Assignment Activity in Log Analytics

Three KQL queries surface blueprint assignment events in Log Analytics via the AzureActivity table: all assignment write events in the last 30 days (who assigned what), failed assignment events with error codes and messages (diagnose stuck assignments), and policy compliance data for blueprint-managed policy assignments. Activity log data requires the management subscription's Log Analytics workspace to have Activity Log connected via a diagnostic setting at the alz MG scope.

// KQL queries for blueprint assignment health in Log Analytics

// 1. Azure Activity Log: blueprint assignment events in the last 30 days
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResourceProvider =~ 'microsoft.blueprint'
| where OperationNameValue =~ 'microsoft.blueprint/blueprintassignments/write'
| project TimeGenerated, SubscriptionId,
    Caller = tostring(parse_json(Authorization).principalId),
    Status = ActivityStatusValue,
    AssignmentName = Resource
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// 2. Blueprint assignment failure events
AzureActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResourceProvider =~ 'microsoft.blueprint'
| where ActivityStatusValue =~ 'Failed'
| project TimeGenerated, SubscriptionId, Resource,
    ErrorCode = tostring(parse_json(Properties).statusCode),
    ErrorMessage = tostring(parse_json(Properties).statusMessage)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

// 3. Policy compliance summary for subscriptions managed by blueprints
// (Blueprint policy assignments show up as regular policy assignments in compliance data)
AzureDiagnostics
| where ResourceType =~ 'POLICYASSIGNMENTS'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize
    TotalEvaluations = count(),
    NonCompliant = countif(category_s =~ 'NonCompliant')
    by SubscriptionId, ResourceId
| where NonCompliant > 0
| order by NonCompliant desc
Common Blueprint Assignment Mistakes
Mistake
Consequence
Fix
Assigning a draft blueprint (not published)
CLI returns an error: the blueprint is not in a 'published' state and cannot be assigned
Always run az blueprint publish --version <N> before az blueprint assignment create
Using AllResourcesReadOnly on subscriptions with active application workloads
All write operations on blueprint-managed resources fail — application deployments, configuration changes, and patches all fail silently
Use AllResourcesDoNotDelete for active workloads; reserve ReadOnly only for archived/frozen environments
Not tracking the assignment identity (system-assigned)
System-assigned managed identity is deleted when the assignment is deleted; DINE policy remediations that use this identity fail after deletion
Use UserAssigned identity for DINE policies so the identity persists; or re-grant permissions after each assignment recreation
Assigning blueprints after July 11, 2026 retirement
The Blueprint service API returns errors for new assignment requests after retirement
Switch to az stack sub create (Deployment Stacks) for all new assignment scenarios
Not specifying principalType in role assignment artifacts
ARM performs a directory lookup to determine the principal type; this fails in tenants with strict directory read permissions, causing role assignment artifacts to fail
Always specify principalType: Group, ServicePrincipal, or User in role assignment artifact JSON
Deleting an assignment expecting resources to be removed
Blueprint assignment deletion removes the assignment object and locks, but ALL deployed resources remain in the subscription
Delete resources explicitly after assignment deletion; or use Deployment Stacks with --action-on-unmanage deleteAll for auto-cleanup
Summary

Blueprint assignments link a published version to a target subscription with parameter values, resource group name mappings, lock mode (None/DoNotDelete/ReadOnly), and a managed identity (system or user-assigned). The system-assigned identity is automatically granted Owner on the target subscription; use user-assigned for cross-subscription DINE policies or persistent identities. Assignment deletion removes locks but not resources. After the July 11, 2026 retirement date, existing assignments retain deployed resources and role assignments but lose lock enforcement — migrate to Deployment Stacks before this date.
